payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming and completion workflow executed by children on a weekly cycle
payload.justification: Signal describes a strategic business process involving child-initiated task selection and fulfillment tracking

payload.description: Task definition and monetary valuation process performed by parents
payload.justification: Signal identifies the parent-driven process of establishing compensated work assignments with financial parameters

payload.description: Performance reporting and compensation distribution process enabling parental oversight
payload.justification: Signal describes the strategic process of accessing child performance data to inform accurate payment administration

payload.description: Task as a unit of work with completion status and associated monetary value
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference tasks as strategically important entities with attributes including status, availability, and compensation amount

payload.description: Weekly earnings summary representing accumulated child compensation for task completion
payload.justification: Signal identifies earnings as a distinct strategic data entity aggregated over a weekly period

payload.description: Task history representing longitudinal performance records
payload.justification: Signal describes historical task data as a strategically important entity supporting accountability and analysis

payload.description: Child claims and completes tasks resulting in earnings accumulation
payload.justification: Signals establish the strategic relationship between task completion actions and resulting financial compensation

payload.description: Weekly pocket money cycle governing task completion and earnings calculation
payload.justification: Signals explicitly reference a weekly temporal cycle as strategically significant for task tracking and compensation summarization

payload.description: Child as task executor and earnings recipient
payload.justification: Signals consistently identify children as strategic actors performing task-related activities and viewing compensation information

payload.description: Parent as task administrator and compensation distributor
payload.justification: Signals establish parents as the strategic role responsible for task design, performance monitoring, and payment execution

payload.description: Enable children to participate in a transparent pocket money system through task completion and earnings tracking
payload.justification: These signals collectively express the strategic goal of empowering children with visibility and agency in a structured compensation system based on task performance

payload.description: Establish parental capability to design, monitor, and administer a value-based task compensation framework
payload.justification: Signals articulate the parent-centric strategic objective of creating and managing a monetized task assignment system with performance oversight

payload.description: Support accountability and behavioral insight through historical performance data retention
payload.justification: Signal indicates a strategic purpose for longitudinal data access enabling trend analysis and responsibility tracking over time

payload.description: Define task with monetary value: parent capability to create task entities and assign associated dollar amounts
payload.justification: Core business function enabling task creation with value assignment

payload.description: Claim and complete task: workflow whereby children select available tasks, perform work, and link completion to their identity for earnings credit
payload.justification: Primary business process describing the task lifecycle from availability through child completion

payload.description: View task availability: child capability to access and review current available and completed task listings within the active period
payload.justification: Supporting function enabling child actor awareness of work opportunities and status

payload.description: Calculate weekly earnings total: aggregation capability that sums monetary values of completed tasks by child within weekly boundaries
payload.justification: Computational function producing derived earnings data for child visibility

payload.description: Generate parent payment report: reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings associations to facilitate allowance disbursement
payload.justification: Business function supporting parent financial obligation fulfillment through visibility of earned amounts

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset: business rule requiring task inventory to refresh and return to available state at the start of each new week
payload.justification: Explicit cyclical rule governing task state transitions tied to temporal events

payload.description: Retain historical task data: archival capability maintaining accessibility of prior period task records for audit and reference purposes
payload.justification: Data management function ensuring temporal persistence beyond operational cycles

payload.description: Task: a unit of work defined by parents with associated monetary value that can be claimed and completed by children
payload.justification: Multiple signals establish task as a core business entity with attributes (monetary value) and relationships to both parent and child actors

payload.description: Monetary value: the dollar amount assigned to each task that becomes earned income upon completion
payload.justification: Explicit attribute of task entities that enables reward tracking and aggregation

payload.description: Task status: distinguishes available tasks from completed tasks within the business workflow
payload.justification: Implicit status attribute required to differentiate available versus completed task states and enable weekly reset logic

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregate: calculated total of monetary values for tasks completed by a child within a weekly period
payload.justification: Derived entity representing aggregated monetary totals scoped to child and week

payload.description: Child claims task: association linking a specific child to a completed task entity for ownership and earnings attribution
payload.justification: Core business relationship enabling task completion tracking and child-to-earnings linkage

payload.description: Parent assigns task: originating relationship establishing task definition and monetary value by parent actor
payload.justification: Foundational relationship for task entity creation and ownership within the system

payload.description: Historical task record: persistent data entity capturing past task assignments, completions, and earnings across time periods
payload.justification: Distinct entity for temporal data retention beyond current operational period

payload.description: Weekly period: recurring temporal boundary defining task availability cycles and earnings aggregation scope
payload.justification: Fundamental time-based cycle governing both reset operations and earnings calculation

payload.description: Task completion: discrete business event occurring when a child finalizes work, triggering earnings attribution and status change
payload.justification: Significant temporal event changing task state and initiating earnings recording

payload.description: Week boundary reached: temporal trigger initiating task availability reset and potentially earnings reporting cycle
payload.trigger_condition: At start of new weekly period
payload.justification: Specific trigger point activating cyclical business rule for task state refresh

payload.description: Historical retention period: extended temporal scope across multiple weeks during which past task data remains accessible
payload.justification: Longer-term temporal dimension for data persistence beyond immediate operational cycles

payload.description: Parent: business actor responsible for defining tasks, assigning monetary values, and reviewing earnings for payment fulfillment
payload.justification: Primary actor role with task creation and payment accountability responsibilities

payload.description: Child: business actor who claims tasks, performs work, views earnings, and receives monetary rewards
payload.justification: Primary actor role executing tasks and benefiting from reward system

payload.description: Track child earnings from completed tasks: business objective to monitor monetary rewards earned through task completion for allowance distribution
payload.justification: Overarching business purpose stated explicitly and supported by tracking and reporting capabilities

payload.description: Child visibility of work and rewards: guiding principle ensuring children can view available opportunities and earned results within operational timeframes
payload.justification: Foundational transparency principle supporting child engagement and awareness

payload.description: Parent payment accountability: guiding principle requiring visibility of child-earnings relationships to enable accurate allowance fulfillment
payload.justification: Business principle establishing parent responsibility for payment based on verified earnings data

payload.description: Weekly task cycle boundary: temporal constraint limiting task availability to weekly periods with mandatory reset at cycle completion
payload.justification: Explicit constraint defining operational rhythm and task lifecycle boundaries

payload.description: Historical data retention requirement: mandated constraint requiring preservation and accessibility of past task records across time
payload.justification: Business constraint enforcing data persistence for audit, reference, or regulatory purposes

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation function computing total monetary amounts earned by each child within defined time periods.
payload.justification: Signal describes temporal aggregation logic as a computational function that processes and presents earnings data.

payload.description: Parental reporting interface function providing aggregated presentation of child task performance and earnings metrics.
payload.justification: Reporting interface represents a logical function that transforms and presents data for parent users.

payload.description: Weekly task availability restoration process resetting task state to enable renewed access and assignment.
payload.justification: State reset logic describes a cyclical process that operates on task entities to restore availability.

payload.description: Task completion association rule governing how completion events are recorded and linked to child entity records.
payload.justification: Association rule defines logical constraints on how task completions are processed and related to children.

payload.description: Child user profile representing individual children in the system with access to task and earnings information.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference child users as logical entities that interact with tasks and earnings data, indicating a distinct entity type at the designer level.

payload.description: Task record containing structured information about assigned activities, completion status, and associated monetary value.
payload.justification: Tasks appear as central data entities across multiple signals, with defined attributes (completion status, value) and relationships to other entities.

payload.description: Earnings record capturing monetary amounts accrued by children through task completion over time periods.
payload.justification: Earnings are referenced as distinct data requiring computation, aggregation, and reporting, indicating a separate logical entity.

payload.description: Task completion linkage associating completed task instances with specific child entities for tracking and reporting purposes.
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes an association rule linking task completion events to child entities, representing a logical relationship.

payload.description: Task collection visibility scope defining which tasks are accessible to specific child users based on access control criteria.
payload.justification: Visibility requirements define a logical attribute governing data access patterns for tasks at the child level.

payload.description: Historical task data retention status indicating persistence requirements for completed and past task records.
payload.justification: Retention requirement specifies a data characteristic that must be maintained for historical task records.

payload.description: Task completion event triggered when a child successfully finishes an assigned task, initiating earning calculations.
payload.justification: Task completion represents a significant state change event that triggers association rules and data updates.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle governing task availability reset, earnings aggregation, and reporting period boundaries.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly temporal patterns for both earnings computation and task state reset, indicating a defined cycle.

payload.description: Child role with permissions to view assigned tasks and access personal earnings information within scope boundaries.
payload.justification: Signals describe access patterns and data visibility specific to child users, indicating a distinct logical role.

payload.description: Parent role with elevated permissions to view aggregated reporting data across multiple children and their activities.
payload.justification: Parental reporting interface implies a supervisory role with distinct access to aggregated child data.

payload.description: Data persistence constraint requiring historical task and completion records to remain accessible for audit and reporting purposes.
payload.justification: Retention requirement represents a design constraint mandating preservation of historical data.

payload.description: Access control principle ensuring children view only appropriately scoped task and earnings data relevant to their context.
payload.justification: Visibility requirements reflect a design principle governing data access boundaries at the logical level.

payload.description: Specific operating system versions designated as deployment targets for the platform.
payload.justification: Operating system versions represent physical computational nodes in the Builder row context. The signal identifies concrete OS versions as deployment infrastructure.

payload.description: Technical requirement limiting platform deployment to designated operating system versions.
payload.justification: The normative signal explicitly describes a compatibility constraint that restricts deployment options based on OS version specifications, representing a technical boundary condition at the physical level.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle boundary at which child actor interfaces display cumulative earned amount totals.
payload.justification: SG542 establishes weekly schedule boundaries for earned amount access. This represents a detailed timing specification for interface data refresh cycles at Row 5.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset cycle defining temporal sequencing for task claiming operations.
payload.justification: SG544 specifies weekly operational cycle timing for task reset, which at Row 5 defines detailed sequencing and timing parameters for system refresh operations.

payload.description: System deployment on iOS mobile platform node.
payload.justification: SG545 specifies iOS as a deployment platform. At Row 5, this represents detailed node specification for iOS platform deployment configuration.

payload.description: System deployment on Android mobile platform node.
payload.justification: SG545 specifies Android as a deployment platform. At Row 5, this represents detailed node specification for Android platform deployment configuration.

payload.description: System deployment on Windows desktop platform node.
payload.justification: SG545 specifies Windows as a deployment platform. At Row 5, this represents detailed node specification for Windows platform deployment configuration.

payload.description: Child user interface actor executing task claim operations within weekly temporal boundaries.
payload.justification: SG539 identifies children as operational actors performing task claiming activities. At Row 5, this maps to detailed user interface specification for the child actor type.

payload.description: Parent user interface actor performing task configuration and monetary assignment operations.
payload.justification: SG540 defines parents as actors responsible for configuration activities. This represents detailed user interface specification for parent actor operations.

payload.description: Child role with interface permissions for viewing available task inventory and completed task history within weekly operational cycles.
payload.justification: SG541 specifies operational visibility requirements for child actors, which at Row 5 defines detailed role-based interface access permissions and data visibility constraints.

payload.description: Parent role with interface access to reporting functions for executing pocket money distribution workflows.
payload.justification: SG543 defines parent operational responsibilities in accessing reports, translating to detailed role-based interface specifications and functional access permissions at Row 5.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.

payload.description: States historical data availability requirement but lacks operational retention schedule or access timing details.
